feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: France reports first Ebola patient as cases in Africa surge above 1,000
entry.summary: France said a doctor back from a humanitarian mission was infected and in stable condition, while experts said the outbreak in Africa could be the worst ever recorded.

entry.title: Why you should be brushing your teeth with your left hand to prevent dementia
entry.summary: The secret to a better brain workout might already be sitting in your bathroom.

entry.title: I Was Fine to Give the Guy I Was Hooking Up With an Assist the First Few Times. But Three Strikes and You’re Out.
entry.summary: I'm not this selfless.

entry.title: People With Eating Disorders Are Misusing Ozempic
entry.summary: New research finds that rates of GLP-1 use and misuse are shockingly high among people with a history of eating disorders.

entry.title: Mutating mice becoming growing problem in New York City, Rutgers researchers say
entry.summary: The study also looked at rats. While researchers say rats are not mutating in the same way, they are still evolving, learning to avoid traps and other extermination methods.

entry.title: Houston Methodist announces $110 million gift, largest in its history
entry.summary: The record commitment was made by the Brockman family through their eponymous Brockman Medical Research Foundation.

entry.title: How young coquí frogs balance the competing demands of growth and fighting disease
entry.summary: A new study tracks how young frogs are trying to avoid a deadly fungal infection.

entry.title: 21 Dangerous Wellness Trends Health Experts Say People Need To Stop Falling For
entry.summary: "Injecting yourself full of peptides is a truly horrifying trend. You don't have to be a healthcare expert to know that randomly injecting yourself with...

entry.title: One common type of fat may increase diabetes risk, while another helps fight it
entry.summary: A new review suggests palmitic acid in saturated fats may impair insulin response, while oleic acid in olive oil may help protect against type 2 diabetes.

entry.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
entry.summary: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

entry.title: Younger Adults May Be Aging Faster Than Previous Generations
entry.summary: The kids are growing up so fast these days, but maybe not in the way you think.

entry.title: As cancer death rates plunge, clear disparities remain, new report says
entry.summary: Black and American Indian/Alaska Native communities have the highest cancer rates of any racial or ethnic group in the U.S.

entry.title: One Stage of Sleep Seems Critical For Reducing The Risk of Dementia
entry.summary: We all know getting a good night's sleep is vital to the healthy functioning of our bodies, but the importance of sleep goes much further than you might realize.

entry.title: If You Can Actually Pass This Basic Sexual Health Test, You're Smarter Than Most Adults
entry.summary: This test can actually save your life.

entry.title: Person who traveled through Chicago O'Hare Airport tests positive for measles; symptoms include rash, fever, cough, runny nose
entry.summary: Travelers at Chicago O'Hare International Airport may have been exposed to the measles.

entry.title: Covid-19 vaccine study that was blocked from CDC journal is published elsewhere
entry.summary: NEW YORK — A study on covid-19 vaccine effectiveness has finally been published after being blocked from a government health journal. The vaccine was found to be about 55% effective against covid-19-associated hospitalizations and reduced covid-19-relat…

entry.title: We Asked Pest Pros How to Tick-Proof a Backyard and They Gave Us These 9 Tips
entry.summary: Ticks may be tiny, but their bite can pack a threatening punch. Keep these pests at bay with expert approved ways to tick-proof your own backyard.

entry.title: COVID-19 vaccine study that was blocked from CDC journal is published elsewhere
entry.summary: A study on COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness has been published after being blocked from a government health journal. The study in by JAMA Network Open shows the vaccine is about 55% effective against hospitalizations and reduces emergency visits by 50%. The fin…

entry.title: 4 Things to Avoid After 6 P.M. for High Triglycerides
entry.summary: Looking to lower your triglycerides? Dietitians say these are four evening habits you’ll want to avoid, from sugary snacks to too much time on the couch.

entry.title: A highly infectious disease has been found in Utah's wild rabbit population again
entry.summary: A highly infectious, often fatal viral disease affecting rabbits has been detected in Utah's wildlife for the first time in four years.

entry.title: Tick bite ER visits rise as patients question testing costs
entry.summary: The CDC's tracker shows ER visits for tick bites at the highest rates since 2019. And some people are getting sticker shock over the cost of testing.

entry.title: Yes, You Can Fix Your Aging Neck
entry.summary: The face may get all the attention when it comes to cosmetic treatments, but the neck deserves love too. Novel technologies that go beyond creams deliver lifted, sculpted necks—without surgery.

entry.title: Mom's Confusion Dismissed as Menopause and Anxiety. Then She's Diagnosed with Incurable Brain Cancer
entry.summary: “It was completely harrowing," Jack Dilger said of his mother's diagnosis, which eventually led to 13 brain surgeries in two years

entry.title: A lethal virus has been found among Utah’s wild rabbits. Here’s what to know.
entry.summary: Two instances of a fatal and infectious disease that strikes rabbits has been found in two Utah counties, the state's Division of Wildlife Resources reports.

entry.title: Woman Donates $90K to YMCA Program for Alzheimer’s Patients and Their Caregivers in Honor of Late Husband
entry.summary: Sally Phillips said the community center provided physical and social opportunities for her husband Ken Titus after he was diagnosed with dementia

entry.title: ‘Frankenstein’ Rabbits With Tentacle-Like Growths Are Appearing Across the US
entry.summary: Shope papillomavirus causes the alarming horn-like growths on US rabbits. It poses no risk to humans, but pet rabbit owners should take precautions.

entry.title: 7-Day No-Sugar Meal Plan for Heart Health, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: This dietitian-created meal plan is free of added sugars, rich in fiber and lower in sodium and saturated fat to promote heart health.

entry.title: CDC’s chief blocked a covid vaccine study. Now it’s in a top medical journal.
entry.summary: The study found that the vaccine reduced the risk of emergency department visits and hospitalizations among healthy adults by about half last winter.

entry.title: Millennials may not be aging as well as they think — study finds ’90s babies are aging faster, have higher cancer risk
entry.summary: A new study suggests that younger generations are aging faster and at higher risk of cancer than older generations when they were the same age.

entry.title: Experts say these cereals are linked to lowering your risk of a stroke
entry.summary: Strokes are considered one of the leading causes of death in America, according to the CDC

entry.title: Developmental issues in kids spike 62% when mom has key health issue
entry.summary: “Optimizing a woman’s heart health before and during the time of the pregnancy may be possibly one of the earliest opportunities that we have to influence lifelong health outcomes for both th…

entry.title: Popular pain supplement linked to faster Alzheimer’s decline — and death
entry.summary: While many turn to a popular supplement to ease joint pain, new research suggests they could actually speed up cognitive decline.

entry.title: Study Finds Many Older Adults Will Improve Over Time–Depending on Their Mindset
entry.summary: The improvements were not limited to a small group of exceptional individuals and, notably, were linked to an often overlooked factor.

entry.title: 9 Fruits Packed With Prebiotics That Support a Healthier Gut
entry.summary: Which fruits have the most prebiotics? Fruit rich in prebiotic fiber to feed good bacteria in the gut includes bananas, apples, blueberries and nectarines.

entry.title: 5 Quick Morning Exercises That Burn Belly Pooch All Day Long After 55
entry.summary: A wellness coach shares five quick morning exercises that address belly pooch and strengthen the core after 55, no equipment.

entry.title: Keep an eye on your vision health with TRICARE
entry.summary: About 11 million Americans over age 12 need vision correction, according to the CDC. Do you know if you’re one of them? Getting routine eye exams to monitor your eyesight can help detect and prevent

entry.title: 5 Drinks That Could Help Support Longevity, According to a Dietitian
entry.summary: Tea, coffee, and kefir may support longevity by lowering inflammation and protecting against cell damage that might otherwise contribute to aging and chronic disease.

entry.title: Sons, brothers and husbands. More men are now caregivers for aging relatives.
entry.summary: Guys are stepping up to care for their parents and spouses as the nation gets older. They face unique struggles.

entry.title: 7 Expert Habits for a Better Night’s Sleep From Neuroscientist Dr. Matthew Walker
entry.summary: The author of ‘Why We Sleep’ has suffered from insomnia himself, and swears by banishing blue light and a strict nightly digital “detox.”

entry.title: I’m an oncologist — 5 cancer symptoms people dismiss as normal aging or stress
entry.summary: Many cancer symptoms overlap with changes people commonly associate with getting older, making treatable cancers more difficult to detect. Knowing which symptoms are often dismissed can help people…

entry.title: Your Taste For Onions May Reveal Something About Your Future Health
entry.summary: Diet and health are closely related, but the details can be fuzzy, with so many variables at play.

entry.title: We Asked a Doctor If Taking Magnesium Before Bed Can Improve Insulin Sensitivity
entry.summary: Magnesium could help improve insulin sensitivity, though you don't need to take it at a certain time of day to reap the benefits. You should speak with a healthcare before starting magnesium supplementation to make sure it's safe for you.

entry.title: With Mass. cutting weight loss drug coverage, patients scramble to afford prescriptions
entry.summary: The impending loss of coverage on July 1 has some of the state’s lowest-income residents, who rely on Medicaid, searching for extra cash to pay for the medicines out of pocket. The costs are steep, typically up to $450 per month.

entry.title: Column | Want a younger brain? Exercise in your 40s may help.
entry.summary: Even if you’ve never worked out, starting in middle age — even with brisk walks — can have a bigger impact on your brain than you think.

entry.title: No. 1 Mistake People Make When Buying Walking Shoes That Harms Foot Health
entry.summary: In TODAY.com’s Expert Tip of the Day, a physical therapist reveals a common mistake people make when buying walking shoes that can harm foot health, and how to avoid it.

entry.title: The 5 mosquito-borne diseases you’re most likely to get in the U.S.
entry.summary: These diseases usually cause mild symptoms (or none at all), but they can have more serious complications in certain people.

entry.title: Want to age better? Researchers say 4-minute routine may help prevent dangerous falls
entry.summary: A Penn State clinical trial finds just four minutes of daily strength exercise can improve mobility, balance, and leg strength in older adults.

entry.title: 8 Probiotic Superfoods Recommended by a Nutritionist to Support Gut Health
entry.summary: A nutritionist and a registered dietitian share which foods help populate good bacteria in your gut.

entry.title: Shocking side effect of blood pressure medication sent woman to the ER
entry.summary: A swollen lip was the first sign of a life-threatening reaction to a medication she’d been on for 10 years.

entry.title: A Common Supplement Shows a Concerning Link to Heart Failure
entry.summary: One of the most popular supplements sold in the US could be having an unintended side effect, research suggests.
